description: Console errors, load times, memory, rapid navigation stress test

tests.name: No console errors on load
steps: Fresh page load
Wait for full initialization
Read all console messages with error level
Count console.error entries
tests.pass_criteria: Zero console.error entries on fresh load

tests.name: No unexpected warnings
steps: After full load, read console warnings
Filter out expected warnings (e.g., deprecation notices from libs)
Count unexpected console.warn entries

tests.name: Page load time
steps: Use JS eval to measure performance.timing or PerformanceNavigationTiming
Calculate DOMContentLoaded time
Record the value

tests.name: Memory baseline
steps: After full load, use JS eval to check performance.memory (if available)
Record usedJSHeapSize
Convert to MB

tests.name: Search index build time
steps: Use JS eval to check if search index was timed during init
Or measure by running buildSearchIndex() and timing it
Record time in milliseconds
tests.pass_criteria: Search index builds in under 2 seconds

tests.name: Large text render
steps: Navigate to Genesis (largest text with 16 eras)
Measure time from click to content visible
Check for rendering jank or freezing
Verify all eras render
tests.pass_criteria: Genesis renders completely without visible lag

tests.name: Rapid navigation stress
steps: Quickly navigate: Genesis -> Exodus -> Romans -> 1 Enoch -> Matthew
Click each within 1 second of the previous
Check final text (Matthew) renders correctly
Check console for errors
tests.pass_criteria: No crashes, final text renders, no console errors

tests.name: No failed network requests (mobile)
steps: After mobile loads and data fetching completes
Check network requests for any 404 or 500 status codes
List any failed requests
